message.content:
I've addressed both blocking concerns. Here's what changed in commit `e55c052`: ## Fix 1: Empty nav landmark (Heydon's a11y concern) - Moved "Create account" and "Sign in" links into `{% block nav %}` so the header `
` is no longer empty - Removed the separate `
` from the content block ## Fix 2: Missing CSS (Steve Schoger + Lea's concern) - Added `.landing-hero` component in the CSS components layer - 24rem max-width matching auth-form for visual consistency - `.landing-hero__cta` -- full-width primary CTA link styled as button (primary color, rounded, medium weight) - `.landing-hero__alt` -- secondary "Already have an account? Sign in" text in muted small type - All values reference design tokens (no hardcoded values) - `prefers-reduced-motion` respected on CTA transition ## Template structure The content area now has: - `

Welcome

` (unchanged, existing test still passes) - `

Keep track of what you need to do.

` - Primary CTA: "Create account" link to /register - Secondary: "Already have an account? Sign in" link to /login ## Pipeline - `cargo fmt --check` -- PASS - `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` -- PASS - `cargo test` -- 67 integration + 30 unit tests all pass - Working tree clean Please re-review and confirm consensus.

content.text: Let me see the full CTA styles to confirm the color/background properties are there.
Everything checks out. Let me send my consent.

input.content:
**CONSENT.** Both blocking issues are resolved. Here's my visual design assessment of the final implementation: **Layout/Centering** -- `.landing-hero` uses `max-inline-size: 24rem; margin-inline: auto; padding-block-start: var(--space-3xl)` with `text-align: center`. This matches the auth-form center-axis and gives the page generous breathing room. Exactly right. **Visual Hierarchy** -- Clear single focal point. The `

` establishes the page, the muted `

` provides context, and the full-width primary blue CTA ("Create account") is the obvious next action. The secondary "Already have an account? Sign in" text is properly subordinated in `--font-size-sm` and `--color-fg-muted`. One primary action per screen -- correct. **Design Token Compliance** -- Every value references tokens: spacing (`--space-sm`, `--space-md`, `--space-xl`, `--space-3xl`), colors (`--color-primary`, `--color-primary-dark`, `--color-fg-muted`), radii (`--radius-md`), weights (`--font-medium`), transitions (`--transition-fast`). No hardcoded values. Clean. **CTA Styling** -- `inline-flex`, full-width, `border-radius: var(--radius-md)`, white text on primary blue background with dark hover state. Matches the visual weight and treatment of `.auth-form__submit`. Consistent. **Motion** -- `prefers-reduced-motion` respected via the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` wrapper on the CTA transition. Correct. **Actions Container** -- `.landing-hero__actions` stacks the CTA and alt text vertically with `--space-md` gap via flexbox column. Good separation between primary and secondary actions. This is a well-designed landing page. Clean, focused, consistent with the rest of the app.
input.summary: CONSENT: visual design issues resolved, looks good

routing.content:
**CONSENT.** Both blocking issues are resolved. Here's my visual design assessment of the final implementation: **Layout/Centering** -- `.landing-hero` uses `max-inline-size: 24rem; margin-inline: auto; padding-block-start: var(--space-3xl)` with `text-align: center`. This matches the auth-form center-axis and gives the page generous breathing room. Exactly right. **Visual Hierarchy** -- Clear single focal point. The `

` establishes the page, the muted `

` provides context, and the full-width primary blue CTA ("Create account") is the obvious next action. The secondary "Already have an account? Sign in" text is properly subordinated in `--font-size-sm` and `--color-fg-muted`. One primary action per screen -- correct. **Design Token Compliance** -- Every value references tokens: spacing (`--space-sm`, `--space-md`, `--space-xl`, `--space-3xl`), colors (`--color-primary`, `--color-primary-dark`, `--color-fg-muted`), radii (`--radius-md`), weights (`--font-medium`), transitions (`--transition-fast`). No hardcoded values. Clean. **CTA Styling** -- `inline-flex`, full-width, `border-radius: var(--radius-md)`, white text on primary blue background with dark hover state. Matches the visual weight and treatment of `.auth-form__submit`. Consistent. **Motion** -- `prefers-reduced-motion` respected via the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` wrapper on the CTA transition. Correct. **Actions Container** -- `.landing-hero__actions` stacks the CTA and alt text vertically with `--space-md` gap via flexbox column. Good separation between primary and secondary actions. This is a well-designed landing page. Clean, focused, consistent with the rest of the app.

content.text:
**CONSENT** from Steve Schoger (UI Designer).

Both of my earlier blocking concerns have been fully resolved:

1. **CSS is in place** -- The orphaned `.btn`/`.btn--primary`/`.btn--secondary` classes are gone, replaced with component-scoped `.landing-hero__cta` and `.landing-hero__alt` styles. All values reference design tokens. No hardcoded values.

2. **Layout is centered** -- `.landing-hero` uses `max-inline-size: 24rem; margin-inline: auto` matching the auth-form center-axis, with generous `--space-3xl` top padding.

The visual hierarchy is clear: one `<h1>`, subordinated muted subtext, a full-width primary blue CTA for "Create account," and a small secondary text link for "Sign in." One primary action per screen, consistent with the rest of the app's design language.
